item.title: Supreme Court hears murder accused Andrew Mungatopi called for help after stabbing sistergirl
item.description: The partner of a man charged with the stabbing murder of a Tiwi Islands sistergirl tells the NT Supreme Court she saw him run outside "like a flash" before crying and repeatedly calling out "call the police".

item.title: Bulldogs cop injury carnage on 'traumatic' night as superstar Darcy tears ACL
item.description: The Bulldogs are trounced by 75 points and lose Sam Darcy to a knee injury as Sydney retains its supremacy against GWS.

item.title: Parents of Aboriginal teen share anguish during sentence hearing of convicted police officer
item.description: The parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after colliding with a police car, share their emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing begins.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Millennials and gen Z clash in season two of Beef, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac
item.description: The dark comedy series returns with a biting look at generational conflict, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as a "combustible" middle-aged couple.

item.title: Iran war and energy shock make delivering the federal budget unusually hard
item.description: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

item.title: As Iran war fallout spreads, the focus shifts to Trump's mind — as it should
item.description: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

item.title: Video of police officer restraining 13yo girl on Perth train sparks excessive force probe
item.description: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

item.title: Why global stocks keep surging despite economic impact of Iran war
item.description: The Australian Securities Exchange is within striking distance of new records as economists issue worst-case warnings of a close call for a global recession.

item.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains tax changes
item.description: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

item.title: Australia banks on Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark in drone warfare shift
item.description: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

item.title: Coorong fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals
item.description: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

item.title: 'Derek' the dingo spotted roaming in Eastside
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lone dingo 'Derek' a concern for some as he roams suburbs in Alice Springs
item.description: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

item.title: Calls for more awareness of functional neurological disorder to reduce diagnosis delays
item.description: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.

item.title: How artificial intelligence is changing business interaction with customers
item.description: Businesses say they are using AI to speed up service delivery and improve the customer experience, but economists warn this may be the "calm before the storm".

item.title: Missing votes bungle caps off disastrous poll for SA's electoral commission
item.description: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

item.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through orchestral music in Adelaide
item.description: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

item.title: How one walk could spark a national conversation on Australia's past
item.description: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

item.title: Lime scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means for commuters
item.description: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

item.title: Frustrated parents of under-16s look for help to enforce 'ineffective' social media ban
item.description: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

item.title: Producer backs pitch for major film studio in Port Kembla
item.description: As the New South Wales government searches for a site for a second Sydney film studio, one of Australia's most prominent producers suggests a regional location is the ideal fit.

item.title: FIFA World Cup fans set for $200 train tickets for 15 minute trip to MetLife Stadium
item.description: A 15-minute round-trip train fare from New York to New Jersey's MetLife Stadium will set World Cup football fans back more than $200 as officials struggle to cover rising costs.

item.title: Do acupuncture and herbs actually help with menopause symptoms?
item.description: Complementary therapies may help some menopausal symptoms, but more high-quality research is needed to understand how effective and safe these treatments truly are.

item.title: Marie-Louise Eta takes charge of Union Berlin as first female head coach in Europe's big five leagues
item.description: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

item.title: The ancient origins behind wreath laying on Anzac Day
item.description: A remembrance tradition that Australians have adapted to commemorate fallen soldiers has ancient Greek and Roman origins.

item.title: Social croquet connects communities as clubs record jump in numbers
item.description: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

item.title: Agricultural, transport industries call for urgent road repairs in flood-hit Northern Territory
item.description: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

item.title: Death of Sandipan Dhar at Joondalup Health Campus was 'probably preventable'
item.description: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

item.title: How Maltese migrants united to shape Mackay's sugarcane legacy
item.description: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

item.title: Skip the takeaway. This halloumi wrap is quicker and healthy too - ABC News
item.description: The only cooking in this simple dinner is the halloumi. It has takeaway vibes and just the right balance of flavours to become a family favourite.

item.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval
item.description: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

item.title: Haas expected to miss several weeks as Broncos count cost of brave win
item.description: Brisbane's horror run of injuries has continued with Payne Haas limping off in the first half of the Broncos' one-point win over Wests Tigers.

item.title: Mushroom murderer's estranged husband Simon Patterson writing memoir
item.description: Simon Patterson, who claims his murderous ex-wife tried to poison him on several occasions, is pursuing a book deal, his media manager has announced.

item.title: Australian tea tree oil industry faces uncertainty over EU ban proposal
item.description: The EU is considering banning Australian tea tree oil after a study raised concerns about potential reproductive risks, a move industry leaders warn could devastate exports and trigger global impacts.

item.title: Power price hikes threaten Northern Territory councils, may cut access to services like pools and street lights
item.description: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

item.title: Dowerin buzzing as Lance 'Buddy' Franklin returns to footy for childhood club
item.description: Lance Franklin has pulled on the boots to help the Dowerin Tigers, where he played his formative footy, raise much-needed funds for lights and facilities.

item.title: Wallaroos beaten by USA in Pacific Four Series after Kansas City storm delay
item.description: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

item.title: Supanova Comic Con attendee killed after car strikes pedestrians in Melbourne
item.description: A young man who was killed when a car struck a group of pedestrians in Melbourne's north-west was on his way home from attending the Supanova Comic Con & Gaming convention, police say.

item.title: Japan, Australia sign deal for first three Mogami frigate warships
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles and his Japanese counterpart, Koizumi Shinjirō, signed the deal aboard one of the warships docked in Melbourne.

item.title: Jessie Ware on how cowboys, sex and parenting shaped new album Superbloom
item.description: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

item.title: Ready for Hard Quiz success? It's time to temper those expectations
item.description: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

item.title: Today in history, April 19: Good News Week premiered 30 years ago
item.description: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

item.title: How the perfect mother myth was unravelled by a family mystery in Italy
item.description: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

item.title: Why Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration is raising eyebrows
item.description: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

item.title: As the world races to shore up oil supplies, Japan promotes a stockpiling model to Asia
item.description: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

item.title: NDIS unregistered provider crackdown coming, as Butler searches for more than $6b in savings
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

item.title: The Australians keeping a century of rainfall records as stations disappear
item.description: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

item.title: The Solomons Six were recruited to a 'catch and kill' squad in WWII — their stories were lost to history until now
item.description: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

item.title: How statue of mythical sex worker 'Joy' celebrates industry
item.description: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.

item.title: 'Old bushie' Steve Hawe has a dream to make his board game an Aussie classic
item.description: Steve Hawe says he never set out to make a board game — but when inspiration struck, he couldn't ignore it.

item.title: Three months on from devastating fires, Natimuk takes stock of challenges ahead
item.description: State and federal governments give Natimuk and surrounding areas an extra $1.3 million to rebuild after devastating summer bushfires.

item.title: Visits to NSW national parks hit record high with more young people heading outdoors, data shows
item.description: The latest NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service Park Visitation Survey shows there were 65.5 million visits to the state's parks over the 2024-25 financial year.

item.title: School's move to restrict 'recognisable' photos of students on social media welcomed by criminologist
item.description: The independent girls' school in Brisbane is now only sharing what it says are unidentifiable shots of students' faces from the side or behind, due to concerns about image manipulation.

item.title: Pale octopus species mysteriously disappears from SA's Spencer Gulf
item.description: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

item.title: Adelaide Crows to give Jordan Dawson time before AFL return, Matthew Nicks says
item.description: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

item.title: Women's shelters turn curated second-hand fashion into fundraising for frontlines services
item.description: Women's shelters are having to get creative to acquire necessary funding such as turning fashion into sustaining frontline support.

item.title: Record earthquake near Cadia gold mine sparks calls for more monitors
item.description: The magnitude 4.5-earthquake near Orange was logged as the region's largest on record, but only thanks to public monitoring data far away from the epicentre.

item.title: Liberal candidate under investigation for roadworks ahead of Nepean by-election
item.description: The Liberal Party's candidate for the crucial upcoming Nepean by-election is being investigated by Victoria's transport department for unauthorised roadworks in a campaign stunt with Opposition Leader Jess Wilson.

item.title: Kimberley diphtheria rates more than triple in a month after 50-year eradication
item.description: An outbreak of the long-dormant, highly contagious bacterial infection diphtheria in Western Australia's north is raising alarm among government officials and experts.

item.title: Young people taking initiative to quit at Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program
item.description: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

item.title: World's largest live export ship sails out of Darwin with 17,000 cattle bound for Indonesia
item.description: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

item.title: Some Royal Darwin Hospital staff use ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim
item.description: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

item.title: Fuel costs no obstacle as West Australian aurora chasers hit the road
item.description: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

item.title: Head coach of the Tasmania Devils Women's VFL side's tarot reading pastime
item.description: Georgia Walker describes herself as a psychic medium, which she combines with her skills as a counsellor and teacher, as head coach of the Tasmania Devils' women's VFL team.

item.title: Fantasy becomes reality for next-gen speculative fiction authors riding self-publishing boom
item.description: Is your dream to become the next JK Rowling or Stephanie Meyer? A new wave of writers is turning to self-publishing to turn fantasy into reality.

item.title: Man faces court charged with culpable driving causing death after Melbourne pedestrian death
item.description: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

item.title: Thousands of dollars worth of belongings stolen from cars shipped across Australia, but no-one held responsible
item.description: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

item.title: Albury artist showcases how Western Front tied small towns in Australia and France forever
item.description: A local Australian artist is exploring the bonds between a regional park and its namesake in France as part of an Anzac Day exhibit.

item.title: Automation is growing at Australia's biggest gold mine — but at what cost?
item.description: At Australia's largest gold mine, drivers are more likely to be found in a control room than a cabin, but researchers and unions say jobs and safety remain significant concerns.

item.title: Socceroos striker Mo Toure nets second Norwich hat-trick in English Championship
item.description: Young Socceroo Mohamed Toure continues to shine in the lead-up to the World Cup, scoring another hat-trick for English Championship side Norwich.

item.title: Couple makes handmade skis out of Australian-grown timber in NSW Snowy Mountains
item.description: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

item.title: Iran's reclosure of the Strait of Hormuz 'disappointing', Marles says
item.description: The deputy prime minister says "every diplomatic angle" should be used to secure a permanent ceasefire in the Middle East.

item.title: Remote NT communities appeal to governments for fuel security assurances, price relief as costs soar
item.description: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

item.title: Love story: I fell in love with my daughter's father two years after she was born
item.description: After a casual fling led to a surprise pregnancy in my mid-20s, I was parenting alone. A phone call two years later changed everything.

item.title: Interview with Richard Marles
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith issues first statement after his release from Sydney jail on bail
item.description: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith issues his first statement since his release on bail from a Sydney jail.

item.title: Demons cling on for epic win over Lions as Freo makes light work of Eagles
item.description: Melbourne digs deep to complete a thrilling two-point victory after a sensational tussle with Brisbane, while Fremantle claims Western Derby bragging rights with a 56-point humbling of West Coast.

item.title: Carlton aware Elijah Hollands was 'struggling in the game' as AFL demands answers after mental health episode
item.description: Carlton chief executive Graham Wright has "at this stage" ruled out alcohol and illicit drugs being a factor in Elijah Hollands' MCG mental health episode.

item.title: Eels stun Bulldogs after having spirit and desire publicly questioned
item.description: One week after their coach publicly questioned their spirit and desire, Parramatta produces a boilover to beat the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs.

item.title: Hazardous material from WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire in Maddington to be removed by Monday
item.description: Schools in Perth's south-east have been deemed safe for students to attend after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire sparked a HAZMAT warning in Maddington, which is still in place.

item.title: Newcastle Jets claim maiden A-League Men's premiership after Central Coast shocks Auckland
item.description: Newcastle has claimed a maiden A-League Men's Premiers Plate after local rivals Central Coast stunned second-placed Auckland.

item.title: Australia's 'lucky country' mantle has slipped — and we're not happy about it
item.description: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: With Iran and the Strait of Hormuz still in flux, China's bet on renewable energy is paying off
item.description: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy, China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

item.title: Can you ask the council to cut down a tree?
item.description: Expert advice on how to handle council trees causing problems on private property.

item.title: Peter and Rosie use power of song and an appearance on The Piano to save endangered language
item.description: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 20: Can you tame our tricky questions?
item.description: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

item.title: ADHD rates in adults are skyrocketing — but by how much depends where you live
item.description: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

item.title: Two-thirds of Australians leave private information open to cybercriminals, Home Affairs research says
item.description: New research suggests Australians are leaving themselves vulnerable to cybercrime through their public social media accounts.

item.title: Netball star Jo Weston calls for pay increase after new broadcast deal
item.description: Diamonds and Melbourne Vixens star Jo Weston calls for a pay increase after a new free-to-air broadcast deal.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closure has Russia poised to capitalise on global hunger crisis
item.description: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

item.title: Is free public transport enough to convert motorists? Research says there are more important factors
item.description: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

item.title: Sydney kids turn struggle with school into ambitious art show
item.description: At an art gallery in Sydney's inner west, a group of marginalised children are staging their first exhibition — and for many of them, it is the first time they have felt seen.

item.title: US delegation led by Vance to go to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, says Trump
item.description: A US delegation will head to Pakistan for potential peace talks, Donald Trump says, adding that he assumes "nobody's playing games".

item.title: How ex-NRL star Zac Lomax fared in first Super Rugby Pacific start for Western Force
item.description: Even if Zac Lomax's starting debut for the Western Force had been disastrous, it would still deserve to be considered a success.

item.title: Fine and mild weather forecast for Queensland as kids head back to school
item.description: As school holidays end and students return to classrooms, Queensland's weather will return to the seasonal norm with warm sunny days and minimal rain.

item.title: Tasmanian Labor says it's 'back' after multiple election defeats, but there's still a long road ahead
item.description: The Labor party's first state conference in seven years reunited old friends and potentially a few "frenemies", but with issues like greyhound racing absent, drama was largely kept at bay.

item.title: Lifeblood's blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people
item.description: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Why JD Vance might have no-one to talk to, plus Israel's new 'yellow line'
item.description: Welcome to Monday and a return to the rhetorical threat spiral we've been watching for weeks. Here's what you should know this morning.

item.title: Araluen seeks to solve mystery of World War I digger C Gordon
item.description: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

item.title: Australia women lose Hong Kong rugby sevens final to New Zealand, men finish sixth
item.description: The Black Ferns Sevens beat Australia's women's rugby sevens team to win the Hong Kong title, marking the ninth time in the past 10 finals that the Kiwis have come out on top.

item.title: Illicit tobacco report ranks NT 'worst performing' jurisdiction, says 'real penalties' are needed
item.description: Smoking is one of the leading causes of death in the Northern Territory. Now, the jurisdiction has been ranked the weakest in the country for how it tackles cheap, illicit tobacco.

item.title: Public health researcher David Thomas wants the NT to introduce laws tackling illicit tobacco
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to shortages caused by war in Iran
item.description: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

item.title: Falling consumption, rising costs and tariffs take bounce out of yellow tail winemaker
item.description: The maker of one of Australia's best-known wine brands, yellow tail, says rising costs and global factors such as tariffs are behind the sharp drop in profits.

item.title: Chalmers to hold talks with states as he flags further NDIS 'savings' — as it happened
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is flagging more "savings" to the National Disability Insurance Scheme as the government seeks to curb growth to about 5 per cent. Take a look back at the day's coverage here.

item.title: ASX edges higher despite rising Hormuz tension — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 clawed back earlier, steeper losses to close marginally higher with demand for retailers and banks outweighing selling in energy stocks and miners.

item.title: Service club wants legislation changed after RSL refuses Anzac Day two-up request
item.description: An ex-services organisation wants changes to Queensland legislation that allows the local RSL branch to reject its application to play two-up on Anzac Day.

item.title: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings audience music picks to the concert hall and people's homes
item.description: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings Australia's most popular classical music, as chosen by audiences, from the radio to the stage.

item.title: Fire destroys two-storey building at Lake Macquarie's Glendale Technology High School
item.description: Fire has ripped through a building at Glendale Technology High School near Newcastle.

item.title: Fire in Malaysia's Sabah state destroys 1,000 homes, displaces thousands
item.description: Thousands of people are displaced after a fire destroys about 1,000 homes in a coastal village in the East Malaysian state of Sabah, the fire department says.

item.title: Plans emerge for country's biggest AI data centre in Western Australia
item.description: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

item.title: Cameron Green victim of miracle stumping in IPL, Cooper Connolly powers Punjab Kings to another win
item.description: The losing run for Cameron Green's Kolkata in the IPL finally ends, but the Australian is undone by a spectacular stumping from Dhruv Jurel, while compatriot Cooper Connolly explodes for the Punjab Kings.

item.title: Massive fire engulfs settlement in Malaysia’s Sabbah
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Touring artists wind back shows as fuel crisis bites
item.description: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.

item.title: Flames broke out in the two storey building early this morning
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Shane Flanagan out as St George Illawarra Dragons coach after winless start to NRL season
item.description: Shane Flanagan and the St George Illawarra Dragons part ways after a winless start to 2026 extends an 11-game losing streak dating back to last August.

item.title: ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury leaving politics after 17 years
item.description: The leader of the ACT Greens and former attorney-general Shane Rattenbury has announced he is leaving politics after more than 17 years in the Legislative Assembly.

item.title: Gold Coast man Leroy Cane remembered for 'huge heart' as Queensland road toll climbs to 95
item.description: So far this year, 95 people have died in crashes on Queensland roads, with 21 deaths since April 1.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to war in Iran
item.description: Listen to ABC News interviews and commentary and analysis from radio programs like AM, PM and The World Today.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith reports to police for first time since release on bail
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith has been seen reporting to police for the first time since he was released on bail after being charged with the war crime of murder.

item.title: Kakadu Aboriginal corporation to take over as operator of Jabiru Airport
item.description: Jabiru Airport had been slated for demolition in October this year, but after months of uncertainty, a new operator has emerged to take over.

item.title: US capture of Iranian cargo ship puts ceasefire at risk ahead of peace talks
item.description: Donald Trump says the Iranian-flagged ship ignored warnings from the US Navy, but Iran says the action is piracy and breaches the ceasefire terms.

item.title: Australian golfer Hannah Green wins fourth title in five starts with play-off victory in LA Championship
item.description: Australian golf ace Hannah Green wins her fourth title of the season, taking out the LPGA Tour's LA Championship for a third time in four years.

item.title: Man charged over alleged plot to import 200kg of cocaine into Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Government investigating after schoolboy dragged down suburban Melbourne street by bus
item.description: Authorities are investigating how a 12-year-old became trapped in a bus door before being dragged hundreds of metres down a suburban street in Melbourne.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: How many games will be shown on free-to-air TV in the Nine broadcast deal?
item.description: Uncertainties over the details of Netball Australia's broadcast agreement with Nine left fans hesitant to celebrate. Now we reveal how many games will actually be seen on free-to-air TV.

item.title: Inquest into shooting death of Sydney police accountant Curtis Cheng begins
item.description: A coroner will examine what authorities knew about 15-year-old gunman Farhad Jabar in the lead-up to the attack.

item.title: US marines rappelling onto Iranian-flagged vessel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Removal of 88-year-old giant sequoias begins in Hobart's St David's Park
item.description: Two iconic giant sequoias, planted to mark the coronation of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth, are being removed from a Hobart park from Monday after their health rapidly declined.

item.title: Potentially 800,000 year old Australite found in WA Goldfields
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA prospector discovers rare cosmic debris in 'the middle of nowhere'
item.description: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

item.title: Soaring airfares and reduced flights force some Australians to reconsider holiday and work plans
item.description: The war in the Middle East has caused chaos across some of the world's busiest aviation regions, with many Australians now reconsidering their travel plans — or abandoning them altogether.

item.title: Norman Griffiths Oval project blows out to $20 million five years after commencing
item.description: Five years ago the conversion of a washout-prone natural field to synthetic should've cost $3.3 million. Project changes and a legal dispute with the old contractor has blown out the price.

item.title: Fast and satisfying dinners for your busiest evenings
item.description: From frittata to one-pot pasta, these speedy dinners are perfect for busy evenings and worth adding to your regular rotation.

item.title: Drivers are choosing EVs amid the fuel crisis but is the infrastructure ready?
item.description: With no end in sight to soaring fuel prices, 7.30 decided to take an EV for a test drive to see how far it could take our reporter, and if Australia's EV charging network stacks up.

item.title: Parilla Premium Potatoes convicted, fined $360k after plane propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm
item.description: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

item.title: For St George Illawarra, Shane Flanagan's dismissal is a new version of the same old story
item.description: St George Illawarra might have sacked coach Shane Flanagan amid a record losing streak, but such struggles have become sadly familiar to the joint venture.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos injury list grows, with Payne Haas to miss up to eight weeks, and Corey Jensen in hospital
item.description: Brisbane's injury crisis escalates, with Payne Haas to miss six to eight weeks and fellow prop Corey Jensen in hospital with a blood clot.

item.title: Tow truck driver fined $5,000 over 2023 crash that killed man on Forrest Highway
item.description: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

item.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child in 1960s
item.description: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

item.title: Rebel Wilson accused of defaming actor with sexual harassment claim
item.description: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson is being sued in the Federal Court for defamation by one of the lead actor's in her directorial debut film, The Deb.

item.title: Fair Work Commission orders major retailers to help truck drivers with rising fuel costs
item.description: The Fair Work Commission orders that retailers, miners and other companies that use truck drivers and transport operators must hold twice-monthly reviews of fuel prices and adjust their rates accordingly.

item.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed
item.description: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

item.title: Cage fighter threatened pregnant girlfriend after killing best friend, murder trial told
item.description: A professional cage fighter accused of murdering his best friend threatened to kill his then-pregnant girlfriend, a Sydney jury has heard.

item.title: Seeking an ADHD diagnosis as an adult? This is how assessments are meant to work
item.description: Who can diagnose ADHD, what a proper assessment should include, and the red flags to look out for.

item.title: Sandipan Dhar's father says someone should be held accountable for son's death
item.description: The father of Sandipan Dhar, who died after not receiving a blood test at Joondalup Health Campus, says there is still a lack of accountability over his son's death.

item.title: Mystery surrounds prosthetic limb found on NSW Mid North Coast beach
item.description: The prosthetic leg has been handed to police after being discovered by a couple while they were fishing on a NSW Mid North Coast beach.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Japan warns of slightly increased risk of mega-quake after 7.7-magnitude one
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.7 struck off the north-eastern coast of Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting about 170,000 people.

item.title: Humanoid robots race in Beijing's robot half-marathon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record
item.description: The winner from a Chinese smartphone maker reportedly completed the 21-kilometre race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

item.title: Tsunami alert issued after powerful earthquake off Japan's northern coast — as it happened
item.description: The 7.7-magnitude earthquake off the north-east coast of Japan triggered tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting around 170,000 people. Look back on our live coverage.

item.title: Anthony Albanese pressed on Trump's comments over Iran
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Inquest hears warnings missed before mother's death in Darwin domestic violence shelter
item.description: An inquest has heard harrowing details of the final moments of a 33-year-old mother who bled to death after harming herself inside a Darwin domestic violence shelter.

item.title: Carlton confirms Elijah Hollands is in hospital after concerning scenes on Thursday night
item.description: Carlton says young star Elijah Hollands has been admitted to hospital after suffering a mental health episode in Thursday night's match.

item.title: Waves swell in Hokkaido, Japan after magnitude 7.5 earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Keir Starmer says he was 'wrong' to appoint Mandelson to DC ambassador role
item.description: The crisis around the embattled British prime minister continues to grow, with Sir Keir Starmer facing questions from MPs about what he knew of the vetting process of Peter Mandelson.

item.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla
item.description: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found in his seemingly abandoned Tesla.

item.title: Live: Trump 'unlikely' to extend ceasefire deadline as Iranian negotiator says nation won't accept peace talks
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he is unlikely to extend a ceasefire, set to expire on Wednesday night, US time. Follow live.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Disabled NZ parrot uses what's left of his beak to dominate rivals
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science
item.description: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

item.title: How to keep your dog happy living in an apartment
item.description: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

item.title: FBI director Kash Patel sues The Atlantic, claiming false reporting about drinking and absences
item.description: Kash Patel denies all allegations in the report, which quotes numerous sources alleging drinking and absences.

item.title: ACCC accuses Woolworths of misleading pricing practices as next federal court case kicks off
item.description: The sequel to Australia's supermarket pricing wars is about to kick off in the federal court as the ACCC takes on Woolworths over what it argues are misleading pricing practices.

item.title: Labor faces gas tax pressure as inquiry heats up before budget
item.description: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

item.title: Australian women have overtaken men in prescribing rates for ADHD medication
item.description: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

item.title: Why Australia's big gas producers haven't jacked up domestic prices unlike during Ukraine war
item.description: After a decade and a half of mostly failed threats from three prime ministers and constant surveillance by the competition regulator, the east coast gas export giants are suddenly putting Australians first.

item.title: From cost blowouts to fraud and benefits, here's everything you need to know about the NDIS
item.description: With more changes surrounding the NDIS set to be announced, here’s everything you need to know about the scheme.

item.title: NRL limits contact in training in bid to reduce head injuries
item.description: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

item.title: From TikTok to Tongan prison: Australian deportee who imported drugs and called himself a Comanchero boss
item.description: Eneasi Taumoefolau's 2024 arrest on drugs charges ignited fears the notoriously violent outlaw Comancheros gang had infiltrated "the Friendly Islands".

item.title: In charts: Australia's relationship with cash over the past two decades
item.description: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama's post-season entrance among leading storylines as 2026 NBA playoffs begin
item.description: The first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs is officially underway. Here are the key things to look out for in each series.

item.title: Administrators for Liberty Bell Bay smelter optimistic buyer will be found, as workers face job decision
item.description: With Australian and international bidders apparently eyeing off the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter, the workers and their families just want to know they'll have a job at the end of it all.

item.title: V/Line commuters let down as buses replace trains during Victoria's free public transport solution to fuel crisis
item.description: Some regional commuters hoping to save on petrol are stuck in the slow lane as Victoria's free public transport offer coincides with rail maintenance along the Warrnambool/Geelong line.

item.title: Students thank Australian Defence Force veterans with postcards ahead of Anzac Day
item.description: Thousands of children across Queensland are sending postcards to former and serving Australian Defence Force members.

item.title: Volunteers who helped find Celine Cremer plan new missing person search in Tasmanian wilderness
item.description: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

item.title: Native title holders say they have been sidelined by Rex Minerals over SA copper mine
item.description: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Anxiety, confusion and a 'yawning gulf' between Iran and US as ceasefire nears end
item.description: Fears are rising that the relative calm of recent days is about to collapse as the fate of the on-off truce talks seems impossible to guess.

item.title: Indonesian fisher says poverty behind illegal fishing in Australian waters
item.description: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

item.title: How a Vietnamese bakery outlived numerous South Asian shops in Sydney's Little India for 30 years
item.description: Keeping a Vietnamese bakery afloat in the heart of Sydney's Little India for 30 years took quick-thinking from its shop owner.

item.title: Organisation provides pathway to home ownership for Canberrans with intellectual disability
item.description: Not-for-profit Project Independence uses residents' disability support pension to share in a property, giving them legal ownership of their home and allowing them to build equity over time.

item.title: Community calls for water quality over quantity, in Murray-Darling basin plan review
item.description: The first-ever review into the Murray-Darling basin plan is underway.

item.title: Video appears to show Dezi Freeman’s hideout after police raid
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down and hand reins to iPhone maker's hardware leader
item.description: Apple has announced the chief executive who took over from Steve Jobs is to step down and has named his successor.

item.title: Live: PM to convene national cabinet to tackle 'long tail' fuel crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has revealed he will be convening national cabinet again in the coming days. Follow live.

item.title: Dean Young named St George Illawarra Dragons coach after Shane Flanagan's exit
item.description: After about 12 hours without a head coach, the Dragons appoint assistant Dean Young to lead the winless side for "at least" the rest of the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Turtles buried in mud at Gwydir Wetlands
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Turtles 'awaiting their fate' buried under mud at NSW wetlands after inflows halted
item.description: Researchers in north-west NSW are wading through thick mud in an attempt to save hundreds of turtles after the NSW government decided to stop supplying water to the protected wetland.

item.title: Live: Woolworths fronts Federal Court on dodgy discount allegations
item.description: Supermarket giant Woolworths faces off with the consumer watchdog in court. The ASX 200 is set to open higher in morning trading after losses on Wall Street. Follow the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Maddington residents urged to stay in as burnt battery product may react with forecast rain
item.description: Emergency services say a fire at the Li-ion Energy site in Maddington in Perth's south-east — the biggest blaze of its kind in WA's history — will be reviewed to inform future responses to lithium battery fires.

item.title: Australian War Memorial curator amazed to find 4 metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes
item.description: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

item.title: US, Philippine and Australian troops begin largest military exercise in 'real-world conditions'
item.description: Troops from the US, Philippines, Australia and Japan unite for the largest military exercise in "real-world conditions" to ensure security in the Indo-Pacific region. But China has criticised the drill.

item.title: Man attacked by 'large crocodile' at camping spot in Western Australia's north
item.description: A man in his 20s has been flown to a Perth hospital after he was attacked by a crocodile at a popular camping spot north of Broome.

item.title: Victoria Police Freedom of Information response delays at all-time high
item.description: Victoria Police is processing Freedom of Information requests slower than at any point in its history, with the wait exceeding nine months.

item.title: YouTubers face police probe after filming inside Dezi Freeman's Thologolong hideout
item.description: YouTubers are being investigated for trespass after filming inside the converted shipping container where fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman hid after shooting dead two police officers.

item.title: Mount Gambier locals hope for rail return following Qantas flight suspension
item.description: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

item.title: E-bike rider dies after crash with Transperth bus in Swan View
item.description: A 50-year-old man dies at the scene after his e-bike is involved in a crash with a Transperth bus at an intersection in Swan View, with police appealing for witnesses.

item.title: New Zealand urges Wellington residents in low-lying areas to evacuate due to floods
item.description: People living in low-lying areas of New Zealand's capital city, Wellington, have been urged to evacuate, as heavy rain continued to drench the city.
